title: skip_number_b accepts 1. (bare decimal point) without error
description: Trigger: json bytes containing a non-target field with value '1.' (bare decimal, no fractional digits). skip_number_b consumes '1', sees '.', advances pos, then loops over zero fractional digits and returns pos at '.'. The value slice returned is '1.' and the next char is ',' or '}' which the caller sees correctly, so navigation usually still works. But the malformed number is silently accepted rather than rejected. Real impact is low (Vault always sends well-formed JSON) but skip_number_b is now public-API-adjacent via extract_path. Fix: after consuming '.', check that at least one digit follows.
